fact: October 24 is exactly 67 days before the end of the year - that's almost 10 weeks of time left to do cool stuff!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now and can hide up to 10,000 acorns before winter hits!
Why do leaves turn orange and red around this time? Trees stop making green chlorophyll, so the hidden colors finally get to show off like they've been waiting all year!
The sun is setting about 2 minutes earlier each day in most places, which means darkness is sneaking up faster than you can say 'trick or treat'!
October 24 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season - the zodiac sign represented by a creature that glows under black light!
Spiders are at their biggest and boldest right now because they've been eating bugs all summer and are ready to find mates before winter!
In many northern regions, the first frost can happen any day now, turning spider webs into glittering crystal art overnight!
Geese flying south create those perfect V-shapes in the sky because the bird in front breaks the wind for everyone else - it's like nature's carpool lane!
The moon around this date can look extra orange or red when it rises because you're seeing it through more of Earth's thick, dusty atmosphere!
October 24 is roughly one month after the autumn equinox, which means daylight and darkness are now seriously out of balance in most places!
Bears in cold climates are eating up to 20,000 calories per day right now - that's like gobbling down 40 hamburgers every single day!
Morning dew gets heavier and sticks around longer this time of year because the air can't hold as much water when it's cooler!
